questions: Identify the major digital resource and volume produced by the joint IUSSP and UNFPA project that serves as the direct lineage successor to the UN Manual X: Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation. Within this resource, locate the specific 2011 section authored by T.A. Moultrie dedicated to the assessment and adjustment of birth statistics (fertility data). Provide the full suggested citation for this specific section, ensuring you list the section title, all editors of the parent volume, the publisher, and the place of publication.

logic_chain: Evaluation and correction of fertility data
rounds.question: Identify the major digital resource and volume produced by the joint IUSSP and UNFPA project that serves as the direct lineage successor to the UN Manual X: Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation. Within this resource, locate the specific 2011 section authored by T.A. Moultrie dedicated to the assessment and adjustment of birth statistics (fertility data). Provide the full suggested citation for this specific section, ensuring you list the section title, all editors of the parent volume, the publisher, and the place of publication.
checklist_width: Section Title: Evaluation and correction of fertility data
Parent Volume Title: Tools for Demographic Estimation
Editors: Moultrie TA, Dorrington RE, Hill AG, Hill K, Timæus IM, Zaba B
Publisher: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP)
checklist_depth: Correctly identifies the resource 'Tools for Demographic Estimation' based on the IUSSP/UNFPA and UN Manual X lineage.
Correctly identifies the specific section 'Evaluation and correction of fertility data' based on the author (Moultrie) and topic.
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to identify the specific publication ('Tools for Demographic Estimation') without it being named, relying on the description of its origin (IUSSP/UNFPA) and predecessor (UN Manual X). It then requires Wide Aggregation to locate a specific sub-section within that resource and extract a comprehensive set of bibliographic details (editors, publisher, location) found in the citation block.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Evaluation and correction of fertility data Author Moultrie TA Printer-friendly version Suggested citation Moultrie TA. 2011. Evaluation and correction of fertility data. In M
rounds.answer_a:
The major digital resource produced by the joint IUSSP and UNFPA project that succeeds the UN Manual X is "Tools for Demographic Estimation." Within this volume, the 2011 section authored by T.A. Moultrie regarding the assessment and adjustment of birth statistics is titled "Assessment of fertility data." The full citation for this section is:

Moultrie, T.A. (2011). Assessment of fertility data. In T.A. Moultrie, R.E. Dorrington, A.G. Hill, K. Hill, I.M. Timæus & B. Zaba (Eds.), *Tools for Demographic Estimation*. Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population.

## References
[1] International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP). (n.d.). *Tools for Demographic Estimation*. https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/
rounds.answer_b:
The direct successor to *UN Manual X* is the online resource **Demographic Estimation: A Manual of Indirect Techniques**, produced by the IUSSP and UNFPA. The specific 2011 section authored by T. A. Moultrie is titled **"Assessment and adjustment of data on fertility"**. This section is part of a larger parent volume published by the United Nations Population Fund. For a full citation, the section title should be cited within the context of the overall manual. The editors of the complete manual were C. T. C. Garden and D. A. Coleman, though specific bibliographic details for the online publication are often abbreviated. The publisher is the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), and the place of publication is New York.

The suggested full citation for the section is:
Moultrie, T. A. (2011). Assessment and adjustment of data on fertility. In C. T. C. Garden & D. A. Coleman (Eds.), *Demographic Estimation: A Manual of Indirect Techniques*. New York: United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA).

## References
[1] United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA). *Demographic Estimation: A Manual of Indirect Techniques*. Retrieved from UNFPA website.
[2] International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP). Demographic Estimation: A Manual of Indirect Techniques. Retrieved from IUSSP website.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the specific digital resource ('Tools for Demographic Estimation') and provided the correct list of editors, publisher, and place of publication as per the ground truth. Agent B hallucinated the title of the resource (calling it 'Demographic Estimation: A Manual of Indirect Techniques') and completely hallucinated the editors (Garden & Coleman), failing the core logic of the prompt.
